Shares of Fold Holdings rose after the bitcoin financial services firm announced it selected Stripe to power its upcoming Fold Bitcoin Credit Card™, which will run on the Visa network. 
The new credit card will offer up to 3.5% bitcoin rewards on every purchase, with no categories or deposit requirements. Cardholders earn 2% unlimited instant rewards plus up to 1.5% more when paying through a qualified Fold Checking Account. Users can also earn up to 10% back with top brands on Fold’s rewards network, including Amazon, Target, and Uber. 
CEO Will Reeves said the card provides “clear and compelling value” by making bitcoin easily accessible, with no staking, exchange accounts, or balance requirements — just real bitcoin earned automatically. 
The Stripe Issuing integration is a major step for Fold, enabling a broader, more reliable rollout of its Bitcoin Credit Card on Visa’s globally accepted network.

author: Blue_Plowman
post:
$TGE The thing about these SPACs is that they almost always fall off a cliff on merger.  Look at $KIDZ, $FLD, $AENT ... there are dozens.  It's not "shorts."  It's not "manipulation".  It's a new company finding its level in a new market. 
 
Once it finds its level, a good company is going to rise from that base.  A good scam will float along setting up for one big push that allows the insiders out. 
 
Either way, don't try to catch a falling knife.  Wait until it lands and then go pick it up. Technical analysis means nothing on a brand new company.

author: SuperHeros
post:
Why We Are in the Early to Mid Stages of the Bitcoin Bull Market**

The measured response to Fold's IPO ($FLD), which features real Bitcoin integration and solid transaction volume, indicates we are still early in this bull cycle. True market euphoria would likely result in much higher valuations.
Historically, bull market tops are characterized by:
- Excessive optimism and FOMO
- Crypto companies achieving multi-billion dollar valuations on IPO day
- Retail investors flooding into the market
- Valuations detached from fundamentals
Fold’s IPO lacks these traits, displaying:
- Reasonable valuations based on business metrics
- Measured institutional interest
- A focus on a sustainable business model
As we approach peak euphoria, we may anticipate:
- Substantial increases in Fold's valuation
- A surge of crypto-related IPOs
- More aggressive retail participation
This observation highlights market psychology based on historical patterns. Always do your own research and invest responsibly.

author: chicoboy
post: $FLD FTAC also announced it has scheduled a special meeting in lieu of the 2024 annual meeting of stockholders (the “Special Meeting”) on February 12, 2025 to vote on proposals relating to the Business Combination. Accordingly, FTAC has commenced mailing to its stockholders a definitive Proxy Statement/Prospectus relating to the Business Combination and Special Meeting. The Business Combination is expected to close shortly after the Special Meeting, subject to stockholder approvals and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions. Upon closing, the combined company will be renamed “Fold Holdings, Inc.” and is expected to re-list on Nasdaq under the ticker “FLD”.
